name: Herrera v. Collins
summary: Expressed skepticism that a freestanding claim of actual innocence, without an accompanying constitutional violation, provides a basis for federal habeas relief, suggesting clemency as the appropriate remedy.
significance: Cited by the government to argue that freestanding actual innocence claims must be paired with constitutional error for habeas relief, and that clemency is the alternative. Raised in the context of whether actual innocence is even cognizable under Section 2255.
